summary_en: ¥0.67 per share (¥399M aggregate). Reinstatement following prior suspension. Effective 2026-06-29. Prior year: ¥0.00. Rationale: operating profit ¥11,086M (488.9% YoY); chocoZAP revenue growth; four core subsidiaries posted record earnings; parent net income ¥1,440M.
translation_en_brief:
RIZAP Group's board of directors resolved on 2026-05-14 to propose a dividend payment to shareholders at the 23rd ordinary general meeting scheduled for June 2026. The dividend is ¥0.67 per share (¥399 million aggregate), to be paid on 2026-06-29, with 2026-03-31 as the record date. This marks restoration of dividends following suspension in the prior year (¥0.00 per share in FY2025).

The company cites improved earnings for the reinstatement. In FY2026, operating profit surged to ¥11,086 million (488.9% increase year-on-year), approaching historical peak levels. The firm attributes this strength to growth in its flagship chocoZAP business, which has driven group profitability, and to four core subsidiaries achieving record earnings. Parent company attributable net income reached ¥1,440 million, affected by certain one-time accounting items.

Management treats shareholder returns as a core priority and bases dividend decisions on long-term value creation, earnings trajectory, and financial stability. The reinstatement reflects stabilization of the revenue base.
